message:
fix: bridge sync pending accounting and CRDT obligation acquire idempotency

distributed/bridge: Extracted mark_sync_pending() helper that sets both the
sync_pending flag and increments pending_ops with saturating arithmetic. Changed
add_child/remove_child/add_task/remove_task to compare collection length before
and after the mutation, only marking sync pending when the operation actually
changed state. This prevents phantom pending_ops counts from duplicate adds or
absent removes. Also added mark_sync_pending() calls to begin_close() and
confirm_close() when local state changed or a distributed transition occurred,
ensuring close lifecycle transitions are captured in the sync accounting.

Added tests:
- pending_ops_counts_only_real_mutations: verifies duplicate add and absent
  remove do not increment, while real mutations do
- close_transitions_mark_sync_pending: verifies begin_close on an active
  distributed bridge sets sync_pending

obligation/crdt: Fixed three bugs in record_acquire():
1. Kind overwrite: previously unconditionally set entry.kind on every acquire,
   allowing a second acquire with a different ObligationKind to silently change
   the obligation type. Now only sets kind on first observation (when None).
2. Terminal counter bump: previously incremented acquire_counts even on terminal
   entries (Committed/Cancelled), inflating the count. Now returns early when
   is_terminal(), preserving the existing state unchanged.
3. Witness downgrade: previously used HashMap::insert which overwrites the
   witness to Reserved regardless of current value. Now uses entry().or_insert()
   with .join() so witness state can only advance (monotonic lattice semantics).

Added tests:
- acquire_keeps_first_observed_kind
- acquire_after_terminal_preserves_terminal_witness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
